rationale: The US-Iran conflict enters its second consecutive day of mutual strikes with Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz — this sustained escalation maintains high oil supply disruption risk directly affecting Polish energy and fuel prices, though it remains a continuation rather than a qualitative leap.

top_events.title: US-Iran mutual strikes continue, Strait of Hormuz closed
top_events.summary: For the second day in a row, the US and Iran are exchanging strikes across the Middle East; Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz and attacked US bases. This is a continuation of the ongoing conflict (day 104), but the second consecutive day of direct exchanges and the sustained closure of the Strait keeps oil supply disruption risk elevated, which directly affects fuel and energy prices in Poland.

top_events.title: Gold surpasses dollar as top global reserve asset
top_events.summary: Al Jazeera reports gold has overtaken the US dollar as the world's top reserve asset, raising questions about long-term dollar dominance. This signals a broader shift in global financial confidence that could affect currency stability, inflation, and Poland's economic environment indirectly.

top_events.title: Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang to strengthen China-North Korea ties
top_events.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping has arrived in Pyongyang to revitalise China-North Korea relations. This strategic alignment could shift NATO's security calculus and has indirect relevance for Poland as a NATO member, though no immediate threat materialises.
